fund.name: Nippon India Multi Cap Fund - Direct Plan Growth Plan - Growth Option
fund.fund_house: Nippon Life India Asset Management Limited

allocations.rationale: Multi cap funds spread across large, mid, and small companies, giving broad market exposure. This fund has consistently delivered over 21% returns for 3 and 5 years with a solid AUM of ₹9,422Cr. At 1.05% yearly fee, it’s reasonably priced and suitable for long-term aggressive growth.

fund.name: Motilal Oswal Large and Midcap Fund - Direct Plan Growth
fund.fund_house: Motilal Oswal Asset Management Company Limited

allocations.rationale: Large & mid cap funds target fast-growing mid-sized companies while balancing with stable large caps. This fund has delivered 26.9% over 3 years and 24.3% in the last year, outperforming peers. Its 1.1% yearly fee is acceptable given strong risk-adjusted returns (Sharpe: 1.22).

fund.name: Motilal Oswal Nifty Smallcap 250 Index Fund- Direct Plan
fund.fund_house: Motilal Oswal Asset Management Company Limited

allocations.rationale: Index funds track the market with low cost. This smallcap index fund gives exposure to 250 small companies with only 0.2% yearly fee — the lowest cost way to bet on India’s next-generation growth stories. Even with a 10% return last year, its 3-year average is strong at 20.7%.

fund.name: Motilal Oswal S&P 500 Index Fund - Direct Plan Growth
fund.fund_house: Motilal Oswal Asset Management Company Limited

allocations.rationale: Provides international diversification via the US S&P 500. With only 0.2% yearly fee and a strong 23.8% 3-year return, this fund reduces country-specific risk. Last year’s 34.2% return shows upside capture. Essential for a globally balanced portfolio.

fund.name: WhiteOak Capital Mid Cap Fund Direct Plan Growth

allocations.rationale: Mid cap funds offer a sweet spot between growth and stability. This fund delivered 27.8% annualised over 3 years — among the highest in category — with Sharpe ratio of 1.36, indicating excellent risk-adjusted returns. AUM is modest but growing, and 1.15% fee is justified by consistency.

fund.name: UTI Long Term Advantage Fund Series V - Direct Plan - Growth Option

allocations.rationale: ELSS funds offer ₹1.5L/year tax saving under Section 80C and come with 3-year lock-in. This fund has delivered 16.9% over 3 years. Though last year's 77.6% is an outlier, the equity tax treatment (12.5% on gains above ₹1.25L after 1 year) makes it highly tax-efficient for 30% slab investors. Aims to maximise post-tax returns.

plan.setup_phase: Step 1: Open a direct mutual fund account on Kuvera, Groww, or MFU. Step 2: Start monthly SIPs as per the allocation. Step 3: Invest ₹1.5L/year in UTI ELSS before March 31 for 80C tax benefit. Step 4: Set up auto-debit for SIPs to ensure discipline.
review_checkpoints: After 6 months: check if all SIPs ran successfully.
After 1 year: compare each fund’s 1-year return with its category average.
Yearly: verify if your portfolio is on track to meet the ₹12Cr retirement goal.
plan.rebalancing_guidelines: Once a year, check your portfolio mix. If any category (e.g. equity) is more than 5% above or below target, sell some from the overweight and buy the underweight. This keeps your risk level steady and locks in gains.
plan.rationale: At 34, you have 18 years to retirement with a ₹40L corpus and ₹1.5L monthly surplus. To reach ₹12Cr, you need ~14% annual returns. This plan balances aggressive funds (multi cap, large & mid cap) with low-cost index exposure (smallcap, S&P 500) for growth and diversification. Mid cap fund adds alpha, while ELSS ensures tax savings. Fund selection is based on 3–5-year returns, low fees, and Sharpe ratio, fitting your data-driven, optimisation mindset.
risks: Stock markets can drop 20-30% in a bad year — your portfolio value will go down temporarily. This is normal and recovers over time.
Mid and small cap funds are more volatile than large caps. They grow faster but can fall harder.
International funds are affected by dollar-rupee exchange rates and global events.
ELSS has a 3-year lock-in — you can’t withdraw that money before then.
plan.disclaimer: For research/educational purposes only. Not certified financial advice.

sip_step_up.description: Increase SIP by 10% every year to match salary growth

allocation_schedule.trigger: Initial allocation based on high risk appetite and long horizon
Start reducing equity exposure gradually after 12 years
Further reduce equity and add debt & gold for stability as retirement approaches

aps.reasoning: Passive-instrument fraction: 30% of the equity sleeve is in index funds (15% Nifty Smallcap 250 + 15% S&P 500), 70% is in active funds (Multi-cap, Large-&-Mid, Mid-cap, ELSS); 0.3/(0.3+0.7)=0.2 weight → 0.2 score. Turnover: only annual rebalancing with a ±5% band and yearly reviews, no tactical churn → 0.7. Cost emphasis: plan explicitly cites low expense ratios (0.2% index funds) and calls cost “lowest”, but still accepts 1.05-1.15% for active funds → 0.6. Research-vs-cost: fund choices are justified by past returns, Sharpe ratios and category out-performance, not broad-market cost minimisation → 0.3. Time-horizon: 18-year goal, phased de-risking starting only after year 12, stresses long-term compounding → 0.9. Portfolio activeness: four of six funds are high-expense active categories whose rationales cite alpha/out-performance; only two are true index funds → 0.3.

pqs.reasoning: goal_alignment: Plan is built around the explicit ₹12 Cr / 52-year retirement target; 100 % equity now, 14 % base return and glide-path exactly match an 18-year horizon for an aggressive investor who wants optimisation; only minor short-coming is no modelling of the research-institute pay-out. diversification: Good spread across domestic cap-curves (multi, large-mid, pure mid, small-cap index) and US market; all six funds from four AMCs; still zero emerging mkts, bonds, gold until year 12 so not perfect. risk_return_appropriateness: Aggressive 100 % equity mix fits stated "aggressive" appetite; projected 14 % base, 10–18 % band is realistic for the blend; Sharpe-oriented selection; risks section lists volatility & Fx; no depression-style stress number provided. internal_consistency: Rationale, return projection, glide-path, rebalancing rule and risk warnings all tell the same high-beta equity story; numbers add up; ELSS lock-in correctly flagged. tax_efficiency: Recognises 30 % slab, uses ELSS to exhaust 80C and explicitly shows 12.5 % LTCG vs 30 % slab benefit; keeps all funds in equity-tax wrapper (≥65 % equity) so gains face only 12.5 % after ₹1.25 L exemption; could have mentioned arbitrage or Aggressive Hybrid as future debt-building blocks but what is chosen is already tax-optimal.
